fault_hypothesis: The expiry comparison on line 68 uses strict greater-than (`now > effective`) instead of greater-than-or-equal (`now >= effective`), so when `now == effective` the entry is incorrectly returned as valid instead of expired.

constraints.intent: When now equals effective, the branch should treat the entry as expired (return None), so the condition now > effective is too strict.
After the expiry check, if we did not return None, now must be strictly less than effective (not equal), confirming the boundary condition is wrong.
Placeholder to instrument the point before effective__1 is used in the comparison, confirming the value is correctly computed.
Check that effective__1 is properly set before the comparison; the real bug is in how it is compared, not computed.
If a value is returned (not None), now must be strictly less than effective; equality should yield None.
